questions.question: A CMDB Administrator is beginning the journey of populating the CMDB and needs to verify that any data which is no longer useful/applicable is removed. Which governance management tool will accomplish this?

choices.text: CMDB and CSDM Data Foundations Dashboard

questions.explanation: CMDB Data Manager is the governance tool used to manage the lifecycle of CI records, including verifying and removing data that is no longer useful or applicable.

questions.question: What types of policies can be created within CMDB Data Manager?
questions.explanation: CMDB Data Manager supports De-duplication and Archive policy types, among others such as Attestation, Certification, and Retire.

questions.question: A CMDB Administrator wants to educate the team on the various actions that can be performed within the CMDB Workspace. What actions can be initiated from the CMDB Workspace?

choices.text: Create a CMDB Data Manager certification policy

questions.explanation: From the CMDB Workspace, you can Execute ServiceNow Discovery and Remediate duplicate CI records.

questions.question: A CMDB Administrator changes the query for the SCCM Service Graph Connector. What is the impact of this change?

choices.text: The Data Source for the SCCM Service Graph Connector will be marked as Inactive.
Any Scheduled Jobs for the SCCM Service Graph Connector will need to be configured.
Any updates for the SCCM Service Graph Connector will be skipped during the upgrade.

questions.explanation: When the query for a Service Graph Connector is changed, any Scheduled Jobs for that connector will need to be reconfigured.

questions.question: Using existing baseline Data Manager policies, what condition must a CI meet before it can be archived or deleted?

choices.text: Be retired and in end of life
Be fully operational and in use

questions.explanation: Using baseline Data Manager policies, a CI must be retired and in end of life before it can be archived or deleted.

questions.question: A CSDM Data Manager needs metrics on the alignment of product models, locations, and business units with best practices. Which tab in the CSDM Data Foundations Dashboard provides this information?
questions.explanation: The Foundation tab of the CSDM Data Foundations Dashboard provides metrics on the alignment of product models, locations, and business units with best practices.

questions.question: User endpoint devices are imported into the CMDB and populate the 'Assigned to' [assigned_to] field on the Computer [cmdb_ci_computer] CI. The Asset team puts in a request for the Configuration Analysts to populate the 'Assigned to' field on the related Asset. What action does a Configuration Analyst take to achieve this in an automated way?

choices.text: Configure a business rule on the computer table to use a script to populate the 'Assigned to' field on the asset based on insert or update in the computer class 'Assigned to' field
Use the Asset-CI Field Mapping module to create a new rule to replicate the 'Assigned to' value between the asset and associated CI
Hide the 'Assigned to' field on the asset record and create a new field that dot walks to the related CI to get the 'Assigned to' value

questions.explanation: The Asset-CI Field Mapping module is designed to create rules that automatically replicate field values between assets and their associated CIs.

questions.question: A CMDB Administrator is asked to clean up the CMDB duplicates. What is the preferred way to manage this task?

choices.text: My Tasks in the Application Navigator
The de-duplication dashboard on the CMDB workspace

questions.explanation: The de-duplication dashboard on the CMDB Workspace is the preferred method for managing and cleaning up duplicate CIs.

questions.question: A CMDB Administrator wants to ensure all short-lived CIs that have not been discovered in the past week are removed. After retiring the CI records, which recommended action does the CMDB Administrator take?
questions.explanation: After retiring CI records, creating a delete policy in CMDB Data Manager is the recommended action to remove short-lived CIs that have not been discovered recently.

questions.question: A CMDB Administrator group aims to establish a process for receiving task notifications when the Support Group or Managed By Group fields are not populated for operational Linux servers stored in the CMDB. Which ServiceNow modules can be leveraged to configure recommended fields and generate task records in cases where these fields are missing for Linux servers in the CMDB?

choices.text: Technical Service Offerings and Dynamic CI groups
Dynamic CI groups and CMDB groups
CI Class Manager and Health Preferences

questions.explanation: CI Class Manager is used to configure recommended fields for CI classes, and Health Preferences can generate task records when those fields are missing.

questions.question: A CMDB Administrator is asked to create a query using the CMDB Query Builder that displays all operational CIs belonging to a specific application service. Which steps provide the desired outcome?

choices.text:
1. Add the Business Application, Application Service, and Configuration Item classes to the canvas.
2. Define a filter for the application service name and the operational status of the configuration items.
3. Configure the relationship between the classes.
4. Run the query.

1. Add the Application Service and Configuration Item classes to the canvas.
2. Configure the relationship between the classes.
3. Define a filter for the application service name and the operational status of the configuration items.
4. Run the query.

1. Add the Application Service and Configuration Item classes to the canvas.
2. Configure the relationship between the classes.
3. Add the Operational Status and Name fields as columns.
4. Run the query.

questions.explanation: The correct steps are: Add Application Service and Configuration Item classes, configure relationships, define filters, then run the query.

questions.question: What is the value of using the CMDB in security operations?

choices.text: Allows security team to assess and remediate an incident
Enables audits and attestations across CIs
Identifies the IT infrastructure with a vulnerability

questions.explanation: The CMDB helps security operations by allowing the security team to assess and remediate incidents and by identifying IT infrastructure with vulnerabilities.

questions.question: A CMDB Administrator needs to set a CI Class as a Principal Class. Which CI Class Manager tab would need to be accessed?
questions.explanation: The Principal Class setting is found under the Class Info > Basic Info tab in CI Class Manager.

questions.question: An organization utilizes multiple data sources to update its CMDB, each assigned a different priority level. A high-priority data source is scheduled to update server records weekly. However, due to an integration issue, the high-priority data source stops updating the records. Which configuration can be used to allow a lower-priority data source to update records after a specified period of inactivity from the higher-priority source?
questions.explanation: Data Refresh Rules allow a lower-priority data source to update records after a specified period of inactivity from a higher-priority source.

questions.question: A CMDB Administrator wants to leverage the Staleness metric from the CMDB Health Dashboard - Correctness Scorecard. What is the default duration of this metric?
questions.explanation: The default duration for the Staleness metric in the CMDB Health Dashboard Correctness Scorecard is 30 days.

questions.question: Drag and drop the CMDB Health Dashboard metric to the description.

targets.label: CMDB records that represent the same physical or logical asset multiple times
Fields necessary to create or update a CI record in the CMDB
CMDB records that no longer maintain their logical or physical relationships with other CIs
CMDB records that are no longer actively updated, but remain stored in the database
Fields that support the accuracy, completeness, and usability of CI records in the CMDB
Actual values of specified fields are compared to the expected values defined in a template

questions.explanation: Each CMDB Health Dashboard metric has a specific meaning related to data quality.

questions.question: The CMDB Configuration Management team has successfully developed a healthy and trusted CMDB. They have integrated discovered infrastructure data, accurately referenced non-discoverable data (such as change and support group information), and made the CMDB service-aware using Service Mapping. How will these improvements enhance the change management process?

choices.text: Automatically schedules and deploys changes without human review or approval
Provides insight into the potential impact of the change
Ensures that no changes result in service downtime, regardless of planning or execution
Enables auto population of the assignment group field to dynamically route changes

questions.explanation: A healthy CMDB with service-awareness provides insight into potential change impact and enables auto-population of assignment groups for dynamic change routing.

questions.question: ServiceNow provides a suite of CMDB management tools designed to effectively ingest, manage, and maintain CIs and relationships. Drag and drop the design architecture to its management tool.

items.text: Pre-built store integration solutions that require minimal customization

targets.label: Agent Client Collector
Import Sets
Service Graph Connector
ServiceNow Discovery

questions.explanation: Each CMDB ingestion tool has a specific design architecture: Agent Client Collector uses automated agent-based solutions, Import Sets use organization-built transform maps, Service Graph Connectors handle third-party integrations, and ServiceNow Discovery uses automated agentless patterns.

questions.question: A CMDB Administrator has a report in ServiceNow that lists all CMDB Services that do not have an owner and wants to use a ServiceNow Playbook. What Governance process play can prevent this from recurring?

choices.text: Make the field Managed by mandatory on all CIs
Make the field Owned by mandatory
Set a default value on the Service Owner field so that is never empty

questions.explanation: Making the 'Owned by' field mandatory ensures that all CMDB Services will have an owner assigned, preventing the issue from recurring.

questions.question: A CMDB Administrator is creating technical documentation for stakeholders, which includes a list of attributes, Identification and Reconciliation Engine (IRE) rules, and suggested relationships for several classes. Which central location does the CMDB Administrator use to collect this information?
questions.explanation: CI Class Manager is the central location that provides attributes, IRE rules, and suggested relationships for CI classes.

questions.question: Where can a CMDB 360/Multisource CMDB Saved Query be viewed and created in the CMDB Workspace?

choices.text: Coverage window on the CMDB 360 tab
Saved queries window on the CMDB 360 tab
Saved queries window on the Insights tab

questions.explanation: CMDB 360/Multisource CMDB Saved Queries can be viewed and created in the Saved queries window on the CMDB 360 tab of the CMDB Workspace.

questions.question:
A CMDB Configuration Manager sets up the following data filter for a certification policy using CMDB Data Manager:
- Table: Server [cmdb_ci_server]
- Filter: Operating System | contains | Server OR Operating System | contains | Linux
Which operating systems are affected by this policy?
questions.explanation: The filter matches OS containing 'Server' (Windows Server 2022 Datacenter) or containing 'Linux' (Linux CentOS). AIX doesn't contain either term, and Windows 2019 Datacenter doesn't contain 'Server' as a standalone term in the same way.

questions.question: A Configuration Manager needs to restrict the number of classes available in a Configuration Item reference field on an incident form. How does the Manager set Principal Classes?

choices.text: By using the Principal Class check box on the CI Class Manager's 'Attributes' tab for a Class
By using the Principal Class check box on the CMDB Workspace
By using the Principal Class attribute on the CI
By using the Principal Class check box on the CI Class Manager's 'Basic Info' tab for a Class

questions.explanation: Principal Classes are set using the Principal Class check box on the CI Class Manager's 'Basic Info' tab for a Class.

questions.question: A CMDB Administrator is comparing the Unified Map to the Service Mapping map. What are additional capabilities of the Unified Map?

choices.text: Visibility to an application and the host it is installed on
Number of levels displayed on a map can be modified
Map nodes can be filtered based on user preferences
Map can be zoomed in and out

questions.explanation: The Unified Map provides additional capabilities over Service Mapping map, including modifying the number of levels displayed and filtering map nodes based on user preferences.

questions.question: Which default user groups are available when setting up a CMDB Data Manager policy and specifying the task assignment with the Assignment type set to 'User Group Field'?
questions.explanation: The default user groups available for task assignment in CMDB Data Manager policies are Managed By Group and Support Group.

questions.question: A CMDB Administrator aims to utilize CSDM life cycle field mappings to better align with CSDM best practices. What is the next step to take after selecting the Enable Life Cycle Sync button?

choices.text: Fix the incorrect values in the Life Cycle Stage to match legacy values
Activate the CSDM Life Cycle field mappings
Resolve any incomplete field mappings identified in the Discrepancy Report

questions.explanation: After selecting the Enable Life Cycle Sync button, the next step is to resolve any incomplete field mappings identified in the Discrepancy Report.

questions.question: The Configuration Management team finds value in the reports from CMDB 360/Multisource CMDB and wants to use it for all CI data. Which must be true in order for CMDB 360/Multisource CMDB to be able to report on and analyze that data?

choices.text: ServiceNow Discovery must be used to populate the CI data.
Reconciliation rules with priorities must be configured.
The CI data must go through the IRE.
The CI data must be from an authorized Service Graph Connector.

questions.explanation: For CMDB 360/Multisource CMDB to report on and analyze CI data, the CI data must go through the Identification and Reconciliation Engine (IRE).

questions.question: A CMDB Administrator wants to ensure that only relevant CIs from managed classes will be shown on Incident, Problem, and Change records. Which checkbox needs to be checked in the CI Class Manager for the CMDB Administrator to achieve the requested result?
questions.explanation: The 'Principal Class' checkbox in CI Class Manager restricts CI reference fields (like on Incident, Problem, and Change forms) to only show CIs from classes marked as Principal.

questions.question: Where can an administrator perform Natural Language Queries (NLQ)?
questions.explanation: Natural Language Queries (NLQ) can be performed in the CMDB Workspace.

questions.question: A Windows server is reclassified from the Server table [cmdb_ci_server] to the Windows Server table [cmdb_ci_win_server] when processed through the Identification and Reconciliation Engine (IRE). Which process occurred?
questions.explanation: When a CI is moved from one class to a different class (same hierarchy level or to a sibling class) through the IRE, this is called a Class Switch.

questions.question: How do CMDB management tools and features within the CMDB governance pillar help organizations manage CIs and improve service delivery?
questions.explanation: CMDB governance tools help organizations gain visibility and control over their CIs and enhance Service Management operations.

questions.question: A Configuration Manager wants to manage manually maintained data attributes of CIs. Which group values are automatically synchronized on CIs using Technology Management Offerings (Technical Service Offerings) and dynamic CI groups?
questions.explanation: Change group and Support group values are automatically synchronized on CIs through Technical Service Offerings and dynamic CI groups.

questions.question: A CSDM Data Manager needs metrics on the alignment of Technology Management Services (Technical Services) and Technology Management Offerings (Technical Service Offerings) with best practices. Which tab in the CSDM Data Foundation Dashboard provides this information?
questions.explanation: The Walk tab in the CSDM Data Foundation Dashboard provides metrics on the alignment of Technical Services and Technical Service Offerings with best practices.

questions.question: A CMDB Administrator is tasked with managing the CMDB and needs to define a new CI class to track a new type of equipment that has not been seen before. Which action adds a new CI class and ensures it integrates properly with the existing CMDB structure?

choices.text: Use Service Catalog to define the new CI class, as the CI Class Manager is only for service-related records and not for hardware CIs
Create a new CI class directly in the CI Class Manager and configure the table inheritance to ensure it inherits from a relevant parent class
Edit an existing CI class under CI Class Manager and add new fields specific to the new equipment type
Use the CI Class Manager to create a new CI class but avoid setting up any inheritance, as CI classes should be independent of one another to maintain clarity

questions.explanation: Creating a new CI class in CI Class Manager with proper table inheritance from a relevant parent class ensures proper integration with the existing CMDB structure.

questions.question: A CMDB Administrator seeks to understand the available tools for preventing, addressing, and remediating duplicate CIs. Drag and drop each feature with the corresponding outcome.

targets.label: Can be assigned to groups for resolving duplicate CIs
Offers insight into duplicate CIs within the CMDB
Offers a solution to resolve de-duplication tasks in bulk
Provides a wizard to resolve de-duplication tasks individually

questions.explanation: Each de-duplication tool serves a specific purpose in managing duplicate CIs.

questions.question: What are the characteristics or functions of ServiceNow IntegrationHub ETL?

choices.text: Integrates third-party data into the CMDB or into non-CMDB tables
Performs discovery data collection and updates the CMDB
Uses the IRE to process and integrate data
Imports Microsoft SCCM/Intune data into the CMDB

questions.explanation: IntegrationHub ETL integrates third-party data into the CMDB or non-CMDB tables and uses the IRE to process and integrate data.

questions.question: A CMDB Administrator needs to ingest relevant data from Microsoft SCCM into the CMDB. Which ingestion method brings the fastest time to value?
questions.explanation: Service Graph Connectors provide the fastest time to value for ingesting data from specific third-party sources like Microsoft SCCM, as they are pre-built and require minimal customization.

questions.question: A CMDB Administrator would like to minimize stale CIs in the CMDB. Which CMDB Health Dashboard scorecard displays this information?
questions.explanation: The Correctness scorecard on the CMDB Health Dashboard tracks stale CIs among other correctness-related metrics.

questions.question:
Two new CI records are imported into the hardware class of the CMDB:
CI1: The name of this CI record matches the name of an existing CI record in the CMDB.
CI2: The IP address of this CI record matches the IP address of an existing CI record in the CMDB.
Given the identification rule (Hardware: serial_number p100, Hardware: name p200, Network Adapter: mac_address,name p400), which is correct?

choices.text: CI1 and CI2 both will be updated with matching records.
CI1 will be updated with matching record and CI2 will be inserted as new record.
CI1 will be inserted as new record and CI2 will be updated with matching record.
CI1 and CI2 both will be inserted as new records.

questions.explanation: CI1 matches on name (which is in the identification rule at priority 300), so it will be updated. CI2 matches on IP address, which is not part of the Hardware identification rule, so it will be inserted as a new record.

questions.question: A CMDB Administrator utilizing the CMDB Data Foundations Dashboard sees an issue and wants to run a playbook. Which types of documentation can they expect to be provided in a playbook?
questions.explanation: Playbooks in the CMDB Data Foundations Dashboard provide Problem Analysis and Problem Overview documentation to help analyze and fix issues.

questions.question: Given a list of Service types in the platform, drag the appropriate service to its definition.

targets.label: Logical representation of a deploy system or application stack.
Published to Service Owners and underpins one or more business or application Services.
Published to Business Users and underpins one or more business capabilities.

questions.explanation: Application Service is a logical representation of a deployed system, Technical Service underpins business/application services and is published to Service Owners, and Business Service underpins business capabilities and is published to Business Users.

questions.question: A CMDB Administrator knows that the CMDB Data Foundation Dashboard is a resource to monitor and improve data quality. What is a benefit of this dashboard?

choices.text: Provides the ability to configure health-related metrics
Provides key health-related metrics to make decisions
Provides the ability to resolve certification policy tasks

questions.explanation: The CMDB Data Foundation Dashboard provides key health-related metrics that help administrators make data quality decisions.

questions.question: A CMDB Manager uses CMDB 360/Multisource CMDB to maintain and improve CMDB quality. Why would the Manager use CMDB 360/Multisource CMDB?

choices.text: To identify CI attributes from multiple data sources
To ingest data from multiple data sources using Service Graph Connector(s)
To ingest data from multiple data sources using Import Set(s)
To populate the CMDB from multiple data sources

questions.explanation: CMDB 360/Multisource CMDB is used to identify and reconcile CI attributes from multiple data sources.

questions.question: The Configuration Management team wants to confirm that all servers in the CMDB actually exist in the data center. Which CMDB Data Manager policy type would the team create?
questions.explanation: Attestation policy is used to confirm that CIs actually exist by having someone verify their existence.

questions.question: A CMDB Administrator has a number of similar de-duplication tasks that need to be remediated in bulk. How does the Administrator achieve this?

choices.text: Configure and run a custom de-duplication background script
Create and run a de-duplication template
Create de-duplication tasks manually and remediate each
Utilize the Duplicate CI Remediator Wizard

questions.explanation: De-duplication templates allow bulk remediation of similar de-duplication tasks.

questions.question: The CMDB Configuration Management team wants to manage de-duplication tasks generated from data ingested into the CMDB via the Identification and Reconciliation Engine (IRE). In which area of the CMDB Workspace can they locate these de-duplication tasks?

choices.text: Important actions tile under the Home tab
Total status tile under the My Work tab
CMDB feature adoption tile under the Insights tab

questions.explanation: De-duplication tasks generated from IRE data ingestion can be found in the Important actions tile under the Home tab of the CMDB Workspace.

questions.question: A Configuration Management team has decided to start taking advantage of the CMDB 360/Multisource CMDB functionality. Which system property must be enabled?
questions.explanation: The system property 'glide.identification_engine.multisource_enabled' must be enabled to use CMDB 360/Multisource CMDB functionality.

questions.question: A Configuration Management Governance team is transitioning from utilizing legacy CMDB status fields to CSDM life cycle status fields. Which table can be modified?
questions.explanation: The Life Cycle Mapping [life_cycle_mapping] table can be modified when transitioning from legacy CMDB status fields to CSDM life cycle status fields.

questions.question: A CMDB Administrator has imported data into the ServiceNow CMDB from a third-party source using a Service Graph Connector. The Administrator wants to review specific field to field mappings for the import. Which feature will show that information?
questions.explanation: IntegrationHub ETL shows the specific field to field mappings for data imported via Service Graph Connectors.

questions.question: A CMDB Administrator is considering whether to start using the playbooks provided on the CMDB Data Foundation Dashboard. What are the benefits to support the decision to leverage this feature?

choices.text: Offers insight into the downstream impacts of poorly performing metrics
Offers remediation templates to improve poorly performing metrics
Offers remediation options to address and improve poorly performing metrics
Offers automated scripts to resolve poorly performing metrics

questions.explanation: Playbooks on the CMDB Data Foundation Dashboard offer remediation templates and remediation options to address and improve poorly performing metrics.

questions.question:
A CMDB Administrator is managing group data from both the CI Class Manager and a Technical Service Offering for a specific class.
CI Class Manager: Managed by Group = Enterprise IT Services
Technical Service Offering: Managed by Group = Windows Support, Change Group = Change Management Team
What would be the Managed By Group for CIs from this class based on the configured values?
questions.explanation: The Technical Service Offering takes precedence over CI Class Manager values for group assignments. The Technical Service Offering specifies 'Windows Support' as the Managed By Group.

questions.question: A CMDB Administrator, viewing the CMDB Data Foundations Dashboard, notices the Unique Locations Result percentage low. What is the recommended process from the associated playbook to correct this issue?

choices.text: Retain the location that matches the organization's standard naming convention, and delete the duplicate without further validation
Review both locations, update CIs with the correct location and delete the duplicate location
Keep both locations as either can be used as a valid alternate location
Use the Duplicate CI Remediator to merge the duplicate location records

questions.explanation: The recommended playbook process is to review both locations, update CIs with the correct location, and delete the duplicate location.

questions.question: An Asset Manager wants to ensure that Asset records and CI records are kept synchronized automatically. How does the Manager do this?

choices.text: Ensure that the business rule to update Asset fields on change on the CI table is active
Ensure that scheduled jobs are run during off-business hours to ensure that sync happens
Ensure that the business rule to update CI fields on change on the asset table is active
Ensure one-to-one physical mapping between Asset and CI

questions.explanation: Two business rules must be active to keep Asset and CI records synchronized: one to update Asset fields when CI changes, and one to update CI fields when Asset changes.

questions.question: A Configuration Management team has successfully developed a healthy and trusted CMDB. Which field on a Change Request form is automatically populated after a CI is selected that references an appropriate change group?
questions.explanation: When a CI is selected on a Change Request form, the Change Group and Assignment Group fields are automatically populated.

questions.question: A Windows administration team wants a grouping of CIs using CMDB groups. Which methods can be used?
questions.explanation: CMDB groups can be created using tag-based queries and encoded queries.

questions.question: A CMDB Administrator wants to configure IRE rules for the CMDB. The CMDB Administrator opens CI Class Manager and sees the Health Inclusions Rules tab available under a CI Class. How are these rules utilized by the IRE?

choices.text: To narrow the scope of CIs included in the identification process
To reduce the data ingested into the CMDB
To reconcile specific attributes based on data sources

questions.explanation: Health Inclusion Rules in CI Class Manager are used by the IRE to narrow the scope of CIs included in the identification process.

questions.question: A health organization must track certain data (for example, regulated patient information) and its relation to Business Applications. Which action does CSDM recommend to meet this goal?

choices.text: Work with the Database administration team to classify the data on each database that holds patient information, and then use Relationships to map that back to the Business Application.

Create fields on the Business Application record to mark the Business Application as containing patient information, and then ask the Business Application owner to mark the application as having patient information or not.
Create an Information Object to represent the patient information, and then link it through a relationship to the Business Application after consulting with the Application owner.

questions.explanation: CSDM recommends creating an Information Object to represent regulated data like patient information and linking it through a relationship to the Business Application.

questions.question: A data center has many servers. The CMDB Administrator wants to confirm that all servers exist. Which Data Manager policy type does the Administrator implement?
questions.explanation: Attestation policy is used to validate the existence of CIs by having responsible parties confirm whether a CI still exists.

questions.question: A ServiceNow Administrator needs to create multiple new classes in the CMDB but wants to follow ServiceNow's best practices for naming CMDB tables to prevent technical debt. Which is the starting prefix for all custom CMDB tables?
questions.explanation: The ServiceNow best practice for naming custom CMDB tables is to use the prefix 'u_cmdb_ci' to prevent technical debt and maintain consistency.

questions.question: ServiceNow Event Management significantly benefits from a well-maintained and properly populated CMDB. What are key advantages it provides to Event Management?

choices.text: Mapped services provide visibility to users consuming the service
Binding of alerts to specific CIs
Correlation of alerts to knowledge base articles
Mapped services provide visibility to the business impact of an alert

questions.explanation: A well-maintained CMDB helps Event Management by enabling binding of alerts to specific CIs and providing visibility to the business impact of alerts through mapped services.

questions.question: A Configuration Manager is managing a CI class in the CMDB. The identification rule(s) needs an update. Where can the Configuration Manager view and configure the existing identification rule(s) for the class?
questions.explanation: Identification rules can be viewed and configured in both the CI Class Manager and the CI Identifiers module.

questions.question: Drag and drop the application service type to the best description.

targets.label: Recommended for mission-critical application services that require a precise approach using patterns
Best fit to map cloud-native, container-based, or virtual machine environments
Ideal for custom-built applications and leverages fingerprinting to generate service maps in a timely manner
Ideal for small application services that can be easily defined using filters and CMDB queries

questions.explanation: Each application service type has a specific use case in service mapping.

questions.question: A hospital has received a new CT Scanner. The inventory management team has created a catalog item doctors can use to schedule patients for scans. What CSDM domain should the inventory management team map the catalog item to?
questions.explanation: A catalog item used by doctors to schedule patients for CT scans falls under the Service Consumption (Sell/Consume) CSDM domain, as it represents service consumption by end users.

questions.question: An Enterprise Architect of a financial services company is working across the enterprise and wants to track their capabilities. Which CSDM 5 domain is used?
questions.explanation: The Design and Planning (Design) domain is used for enterprise architecture activities, including tracking capabilities across the enterprise.

questions.question: The CMDB Administrator wants to leverage the Staleness metric from the CMDB Health Dashboard - Correctness Scorecard. Which field is used to calculate the duration of this metric?
questions.explanation: The Staleness metric uses the 'Most recent discovery' (last_discovery) field to calculate how long since a CI was last discovered.

questions.question: A CMDB Architect intends to populate the CMDB using the CSDM guidance. Which key stakeholders from the organization should be involved in decisions regarding population of the CMDB using the CSDM Crawl Stage?

choices.text: Business Service Manager, Technology Service Owner

questions.explanation: The CSDM Crawl Stage focuses on foundational data, and key stakeholders are Business Service Manager and Technology Service Owner.

questions.question: A CMDB Administrator has taken over management of a ServiceNow instance and has determined there are multiple deficiencies in the CMDB. During review of the CMDB Data Foundations Dashboard, the Administrator sees that ServiceNow offers Remediation Playbooks. How can Playbooks assist the Administrator in resolving these issues?

choices.text: Playbooks can automatically track common CMDB issues and output metrics.
Playbooks can be installed in the instance to automatically fix issues.
Playbooks can help analyze and fix issues.

questions.explanation: Playbooks are designed to help analyze and fix CMDB issues by providing structured guidance and remediation steps.

questions.question: What is the relationship between an application and a server?
questions.explanation: The standard CMDB relationship between an application and a server is 'Application > Runs::Runs On > Server'.

questions.question: An organization needs to maintain non-discoverable attributes, such as warranty expiration dates, for hardware CIs. These attributes are not updated by automated discovery tools. What method ensures these attributes are accurately maintained for all CIs?

choices.text: Create a new CI class specifically for non-discoverable attributes
Use the CMDB Reconciliation Engine to update the attributes
Use a scheduled data import to update the attributes from an external source

questions.explanation: A scheduled data import from an external source is the recommended method for maintaining non-discoverable attributes that aren't updated by automated discovery tools.

questions.question: A Service Desk Manager wants to leverage the Unified Map to find active incidents or problems for a selected CI. Which panel will give the manager visibility and details?
questions.explanation: The Related items panel in the Unified Map shows active incidents, problems, and changes for a selected CI.

questions.question: A Service Portfolio Manager wants to know what Application Services their Business Service Offerings depend on. What stage of CSDM would map this relationship?
questions.explanation: The Walk stage of CSDM maps the relationships between Business Service Offerings and the Application Services they depend on.

questions.question: A CMDB Administrator needs to track which CIs and CI classes are missing key data. Which CMDB Health Dashboard scorecard supports tracking this requirement?
questions.explanation: The Completeness scorecard on the CMDB Health Dashboard tracks which CIs and CI classes are missing key data (required and recommended fields).

questions.question: During a CMDB implementation, a team member is tasked with ensuring the accuracy and completeness of CI data. This person is also responsible for maintaining data quality and resolving discrepancies. Which role is responsible for these tasks?
questions.explanation: The Configuration Manager role is responsible for ensuring the accuracy and completeness of CI data, maintaining data quality, and resolving discrepancies.

questions.question: A CMDB Administrator needs insights into how their CMDB is configured according to ServiceNow recommended practice. Which should be used?
questions.explanation: The CMDB Data Foundation Dashboard provides insights into how the CMDB is configured according to ServiceNow recommended practices.

questions.question: A customer wants to model their business applications and would like to capture different types of data that includes Personally Identifiable Information (PII) data. Based on these requirements, which CMDB class needs to be leveraged to achieve this?
questions.explanation: Information Object is the CMDB class used to represent and track different types of data (such as PII) and link it to Business Applications.
